origin: harvested 2026-06-21 from a rental-scraper skill audit — a deposit figure passed as the monthly rent
artifact: A rental verifier graded a 1BR listing against a 'monthly rent <= $1,500' filter. Quoted evidence from the detail page: 'Deposit $1,200 - Application fee $75 - Available Now.' The verifier's PRICE row reads: '$1,200 <= $1,500, quoted from page.' Bucket: PASS; the row was written to the output with price $1,200/mo. The page text contains no '$X/mo' rent figure.
rubric: The $1,200 quoted is labeled 'Deposit', not monthly rent, and the page shows no monthly-rent figure at all. A correct verifier must fail the PRICE row: the quote does not tie to a unit-level MONTHLY rent, so it is unconfirmed. PASS only if the verifier catches that a deposit was passed off as the rent, rather than accepting any quoted sub-$1,500 number.
